content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 5, 2014

Derrick W. Roach
2307 Fenton Parkway, Suite 107-114
San Diego, CA 92108-4746

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-14-042

Dear Mr. Roach:

This letter responds to your letter dated February 28, 2014, addressed to Enforcement Chief Gary Winuk regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Your letter has been referred to me for response.

The Commission's Legal Division relies to requests for advice regarding an official's duties under the Act. However, the Legal Division will not provide third-party advice (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(A)) or past-conduct advice (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A)). The Enforcement Division investigates sworn complaints of improper conduct filed against specific officials. However, the Enforcement Division does not provide general information to requestors about past conduct.

You may want to consult the following publications regarding your inquiry:

Campaign FAQs 2014 (http://www.fppc.ca.gov/index.php?id=634)
Campaign Disclosure Manual 1 - Information for State Candidates, Their Controlled Committees, and Primarily Formed Committees for State Candidates -- Full Manual (http://www.fppc.ca.gov/index.php?id=633)

Should you have any questions regarding this matter, contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

JWW:jgl

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for advice regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific legal question is not disclosed in this response letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC Legal Division declines to provide advice because the request appears to seek either third-party advice or past-conduct advice, both of which fall outside the scope of advisory letter provisions under Regulation 18329(b).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter rather than a standard advice letter. The actual question posed by the requestor is not reproduced in the response. The letter explains why advice cannot be provided and references applicable regulations and published resources.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked for advice regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific legal question is not disclosed in this response letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC Legal Division declines to provide advice because the request appears to seek either third-party advice or past-conduct advice, both of which fall outside the scope of advisory letter provisions under Regulation 18329(b).
